perturbation.original_text: 1.6 "Enhancement" means a Vert-General Release Enhancement or a Converge-Requested Enhancement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Converge-Requested Enhancement' from the definition of 'Enhancement', sections of the contract that still refer to or depend on 'Converge-Requested Enhancement' are now internally contradictory. Specifically, Section 3.10 and other sections referring to Converge-Requested Enhancements become ambiguous and difficult to enforce. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because, even though 'Converge-Requested Enhancement' is no longer included in the general definition of 'Enhancement', the term continues to be used in later sections without a clear tie back to the main definitions, creating doubt.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.10 Acceptance. Each Converge-Requested Enhancement provided to Converge under a Final SOW will be subject to acceptance testing by Converge in accordance with the provisions of its corresponding Final SOW. All other Converge-Requested Enhancements provided to Converge under this Agreement will be deemed accepted by 8 Converge upon Converge's receipt of the complete delivery thereof to a Converge computer located at a Converge place of business.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Enhancement' to include 'Converge-Requested Enhancement'. The changed text removes 'Converge-Requested Enhancement' from the definition. Section 3.10 specifically refers to 'Converge-Requested Enhancement' and outlines acceptance testing procedures. By removing 'Converge-Requested Enhancement' from the definition of 'Enhancement', Section 3.10 now uses a term that is no longer clearly defined within the contract, creating an in-text contradiction and ambiguity regarding the acceptance process for Converge-Requested Enhancements, therefore the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: 1.16 "Problem" means a Severity Level 1, 2, 3 or 4 problem with a Supported Product (whether or not attributable or believed to be attributable to an Error), as such problems are described in greater detail in Exhibit A hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.16 "Problem" means a Severity Level 1, 2, 3 or 4 problem with a Supported Product (whether or not attributable or believed to be attributable to an Error).
perturbation.explanation: Removing the reference to 'Exhibit A hereto' where severity levels are defined introduces an in-text contradiction. While Section 1.16 defines what a problem is, it is also indicating that it has a defined level of severity which can be read in the attached Exhibit A. By removing this reference and not the severity descriptions in Section 2.3, it creates uncertainty as to what constitutes each Severity Level, hindering the clear understanding of VNE's obligations. If a Problem is reported, it is now uncertain how to classify and handle it according to the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 Method of Reporting. All Problem Reports and Support Requests shall be communicated by the Converge Support Personnel to VNE as follows: (1) via telephone at 1-877-249-1423, or such other telephone number(s) as VNE may provide to Converge from time to time, or (2) via electronic mail to support.solutions@verticalnet.com, or such other e-mail address(es) as VNE may provide to Converge from time to time, or (3) in person to VNE Service Personnel (if any) on Converge premises at the time; provided, however, that the Converge Support Personnel shall communicate all Severity Level 1 and Severity Level 2 Problems to VNE via method (1) or method (3) above. Converge shall classify all Problems reported to VNE according to the Problem Severity Levels listed in Exhibit A attached hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the reference to 'Exhibit A hereto' from the definition of 'Problem,' while the contradicted text, Section 2.3, explicitly states that 'Converge shall classify all Problems reported to VNE according to the Problem Severity Levels listed in Exhibit A attached hereto.' This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text suggests that the severity levels might not be found in Exhibit A anymore, while Section 2.3 relies on Exhibit A for classifying problem severity. This omission creates a direct conflict and makes it unclear how to classify problems, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes.

perturbation.original_text: 2.6 Response to Problem Reports. VNE shall use its reasonable best efforts to provide Converge with an initial response to and status reports for all Problems reported by Converge, and to resolve all Problems identified, in accordance with the provisions of Exhibit B attached hereto; provided, however, that VNE shall have no further obligation to respond to or attempt to diagnose or resolve a Problem once it is determined not to be attributable to Errors. VNE will review all Problem Requests submitted by Converge at the Problem Severity Level indicated by Converge unless another Problem Severity Level is clearly warranted. In the event of a reasonable uncertainty, the parties will assume a higher Severity Level for a Problem until they have sufficient information to make a determination that a lower Severity Level is warranted. If, at Converge's request, VNE responds to a Problem at a Severity Level that is higher than what proves to be the actual Severity Level of the Problem, or if VNE's review of a Problem reported by Converge establishes that the Problem was not due to an Error, Converge shall pay or reimburse VNE for all incremental fees and expenses reasonably incurred by VNE in performing such activities.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.6 Response to Problem Reports. VNE shall use its reasonable best efforts to provide Converge with an initial response to and status reports for all Problems reported by Converge, and to resolve all Problems identified; provided, however, that VNE shall have no further obligation to respond to or attempt to diagnose or resolve a Problem once it is determined not to be attributable to Errors. VNE will review all Problem Requests submitted by Converge at the Problem Severity Level indicated by Converge unless another Problem Severity Level is clearly warranted. In the event of a reasonable uncertainty, the parties will assume a higher Severity Level for a Problem until they have sufficient information to make a determination that a lower Severity Level is warranted. If, at Converge's request, VNE responds to a Problem at a Severity Level that is higher than what proves to be the actual Severity Level of the Problem, or if VNE's review of a Problem reported by Converge establishes that the Problem was not due to an Error, Converge shall pay or reimburse VNE for all incremental fees and expenses reasonably incurred by VNE in performing such activities.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'in accordance with the provisions of Exhibit B attached hereto', where 'Problem Response and Resolution Efforts' are described based on Severity Level, an in-text contradiction is created. Section 2.6 mentions 'Problem Severity Level' later in the text. However, the detailed specifications for resolution efforts based on those levels have been removed. This creates confusion and uncertainty about VNE's exact responsibilities and timelines for resolving different severity level problems, contradicting later statements about responding at the 'Problem Severity Level'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.6 Response to Problem Reports. VNE shall use its reasonable best efforts to provide Converge with an initial response to and status reports for all Problems reported by Converge, and to resolve all Problems identified; provided, however, that VNE shall have no further obligation to respond to or attempt to diagnose or resolve a Problem once it is determined not to be attributable to Errors. VNE will review all Problem Requests submitted by Converge at the Problem Severity Level indicated by Converge unless another Problem Severity Level is clearly warranted. In the event of a reasonable uncertainty, the parties will assume a higher Severity Level for a Problem until they have sufficient information to make a determination that a lower Severity Level is warranted. If, at Converge's request, VNE responds to a Problem at a Severity Level that is higher than what proves to be the actual Severity Level of the Problem, or if VNE's review of a Problem reported by Converge establishes that the Problem was not due to an Error, Converge shall pay or reimburse VNE for all incremental fees and expenses reasonably incurred by VNE in performing such activities.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'in accordance with the provisions of Exhibit B attached hereto' from Section 2.6 creates a contradiction. While the section still references 'Problem Severity Level,' the absence of Exhibit B, which presumably details resolution efforts based on these levels, makes it unclear how VNE should respond to problems of varying severity. This contradicts the later statements about responding at the 'Problem Severity Level' within the same section, as the specific requirements for each severity level are no longer defined within the contract itself. Without Exhibit B, the obligation to review 'Problem Requests submitted by Converge at the Problem Severity Level' becomes ambiguous and potentially unenforceable, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it removes essential language from the section to define the obligations.
